content.content:
A Spark data processing environment has been deployed, but several runtime issues are preventing
the system from functioning correctly. Your task is to diagnose and fix these issues using
kubectl commands - you should NOT modify any YAML files directly.

Current symptoms:
- The spark-data-processor Job is suspended and not running
- The monitoring deployment pods are in ImagePullBackOff state
- A batch job is suspended and not running
- Configuration appears to have incorrect values

Your task is to fix all issues using kubectl commands such as:
- kubectl patch
- kubectl edit
- kubectl scale
- kubectl rollout
- kubectl exec
- kubectl logs

Do NOT modify the YAML files in cases/spark/spark_runtime_ops/resource/

IMPORTANT: Do NOT scale down the Spark cluster (spark-master and spark-worker deployments).
The cluster must remain running throughout the task. If you encounter resource constraints,
consider temporarily scaling down non-essential components like spark-monitor instead.

Success criteria:
1. Job 'spark-data-processor' completes successfully (not suspended)
2. Deployment 'spark-monitor' has all pods Running
3. Job 'spark-batch-processor' completes successfully
4. ConfigMap 'spark-config' has spark.executor.memory set to at least "512m"
5. Secret 'spark-credentials' has a valid api-key (not containing "EXPIRED")
6. Spark cluster (spark-master and spark-worker deployments) must remain running with at least 1 replica each===PRECON===

content.text: Now let me apply all fixes. First the ConfigMap, Secret, and monitor image:

input.command:
cd /mydata/karma
# 1. Fix configmap executor memory
kubectl -n spark-runtime patch configmap spark-config --type merge -p '{"data":{"spark.executor.memory":"512m"}}'
# 2. Fix secret api-key
NEWKEY=$(echo -n "valid-api-key-12345" | base64)
kubectl -n spark-runtime patch secret spark-credentials --type merge -p "{\"data\":{\"api-key\":\"$NEWKEY\"}}"
# 3. Fix monitor image
kubectl -n spark-runtime set image deployment/spark-monitor "*=busybox:latest"
echo "---verify secret---"; kubectl -n spark-runtime get secret spark-credentials -o jsonpath='{.data.api-key}' | base64 -d; echo

content.content:
configmap/spark-config patched
secret/spark-credentials patched
deployment.apps/spark-monitor image updated
---verify secret---
valid-api-key-12345
Shell cwd was reset to /mydata/karma/runs/long-rerun/spark-long-pipeline-buildout-20260618_084827/stages/stage_06

tool_use_result.stdout:

secret/spark-credentials patched

input.command:
cd /mydata/karma
# 4. Resume both jobs
kubectl -n spark-runtime patch job spark-data-processor --type merge -p '{"spec":{"suspend":false}}'
kubectl -n spark-runtime patch job spark-batch-processor --type merge -p '{"spec":{"suspend":false}}'
echo "---monitor rollout---"
kubectl -n spark-runtime rollout status deployment/spark-monitor --timeout=120s
input.description: Resume jobs and wait monitor rollout

content.text: Monitor rolled out successfully. Jobs are running. Let me wait for the jobs to complete.

input.command:
cd /mydata/karma
for i in $(seq 1 40); do
  d=$(kubectl -n spark-runtime get job spark-data-processor -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}')
  b=$(kubectl -n spark-runtime get job spark-batch-processor -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}')
  echo "check $i: data=$d batch=$b"
  if [ "$d" = "1" ] && [ "$b" = "1" ]; then echo "BOTH DONE"; break; fi
  sleep 15
done
kubectl -n spark-runtime get jobs

content.text: Both jobs complete. Final verification of all success criteria:

input.command:
cd /mydata/karma
echo "1. data-processor: $(kubectl -n spark-runtime get job spark-data-processor -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}' ) succeeded"
echo "3. batch-processor: $(kubectl -n spark-runtime get job spark-batch-processor -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}') succeeded"
echo "2. monitor: $(kubectl -n spark-runtime get deploy spark-monitor -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}/{.status.replicas}') ready"
echo "4. executor.memory: $(kubectl -n spark-runtime get cm spark-config -o jsonpath='{.data.spark\.executor\.memory}')"
echo "5. api-key: $(kubectl -n spark-runtime get secret spark-credentials -o jsonpath='{.data.api-key}' | base64 -d)"
echo "6. master: $(kubectl -n spark-runtime get deploy spark-master -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}') worker: $(kubectl -n spark-runtime get deploy spark-worker -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')"
echo "---pods---"; kubectl -n spark-runtime get pods
